projects.title: HealthHub
projects.subtitle: Healthcare provider discovery and medical literacy web application

expanded.description: HealthHub is a Progressive Web App built to improve healthcare access for rural and underserved communities. I led all development and UX design within a four-person team, building a platform that lets users search for nearby providers by specialty and ZIP code, explore results on an interactive map, and access clear health literacy content. See the case study for full technical detail.
expanded.role: HealthHub was developed as a team project with 13 contributors. I led and owned the majority of the application’s development, including site design, frontend implementation, API integration, client-side filtering logic, and completion of the interactive map feature. I was responsible for translating project requirements into a cohesive, functional prototype.
highlights: Pivoted data source mid-development from Google Places API to NPPES/CMS APIs for richer, more structured provider data
Built provider filtering and deduplication logic to surface clean, relevant results from a large government dataset
Implemented offline fallback functionality using a locally cached JSON dataset for low-connectivity environments
Led all UX design independently, scoring 29/30 on the team's design evaluation rubric
expanded.outcome: HealthHub was delivered as a fully functional prototype for a humanitarian engineering course. The project demonstrates my ability to take ownership of a complex web application, handle real-world data, and balance technical problem-solving with thoughtful user experience design.

expanded.description: Shipped two production Jira tickets to bathandbodyworks.com, refactoring homepage modules from carousel layouts to overflow scroll and resolving a cross-browser scrolling bug where Mac trackpad gestures triggered unintended browser navigation. Worked within Salesforce Commerce Cloud and an established modular design system, collaborating with UX designers and QA through full sprint and testing cycles.
highlights: Implemented accessibility and usability improvements across production pages
Modified existing components within established design and content systems
Worked with Salesforce-integrated content and structured data
Identified and resolved UI issues affecting responsiveness and consistency
Contributed code that was deployed to live production environments

expanded.description: This project involved developing a Unix shell in C that supports command execution, signal handling, and command history management. The shell was designed to closely reflect real operating system behavior, strengthening my understanding of low-level system interactions.

This project involved designing and implementing a relational database and backend integration for an equipment rental company serving a specialized community, with equipment delivery handled via drones. The system was built to support structured data storage, efficient querying, and backend API usage.
highlights: Designed normalized relational schema using MySQL
Implemented primary and foreign key relationships
Created indexes to improve query performance
Wrote complex SQL queries using joins, aggregations, and constraints
